A test to verify that the type of `void 0` is "undefined".

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion that checks the type of the expression `void 0` to ensure it is `"undefined"`. The `assert` function is used to verify that the expression evaluates to `true`, which confirms that the `void` operator correctly returns `undefined` when applied to the numeric literal `0`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test or validation to ensure the expected behavior of the `void` operator in JavaScript.
